title: Technical Debt Cleanup - Complete Codebase Refactoring
description:
Master epic tracking the comprehensive technical debt cleanup based on specs/codebase-cleanup.md

This epic encompasses:
- Quick wins (gofmt, constants, indices, Keychain removal)
- Foundation (Go tooling, CI setup, baseline metrics)
- Testing infrastructure (wrapper, cron, session tests)
- Context/timeout management (git ops, database ops)
- Security hardening (file permissions, config validation)  
- Major refactors (database.go, wrapper.go, config files, cmd/ org)
- API improvements (race condition, error semantics, interfaces)
- Performance optimizations (caching, heartbeat, JSONL scanner)

Total beads: 28 (agenc-323 through agenc-350)

Critical path:
1. Fix failing tests (BLOCKS EVERYTHING)
2. Set up Go tooling (PREVENTS REGRESSION)
3. Add integration tests (ENABLES SAFE REFACTORING)
4. Major refactors (database, wrapper)
5. API standardization and performance tuning

Timeline: 3-4 months for complete execution
Team: Can be parallelized across multiple agents

title: Capture 'thin CLI, thick server' guidance in AgenC CLAUDE.md
description:
There is an architectural principle in AgenC that the CLI should be thin and the server should be thick — logic that previously lived in the CLI was deliberately pushed down into the server. This decision should be captured in AgenC's CLAUDE.md so future agents working on the repo respect it instead of accidentally fattening the CLI again.

Outcome: a section in AgenC's CLAUDE.md (or wherever architectural principles are documented) that names the principle, summarizes the reasoning, and points future agents to relevant past work.

Investigation hints (from `agenc mission search 'thin CLI'`):
- Mission 2703959f ('Locating spawned mission 56c83eb5 to attach') — explicitly framed the principle: 'thin CLI, thick server'.
- Mission fea550a7 ('Cut new minor version release') — release notes mention 'Thin-CLI refactor: tmux session resolution moved from CLI to server', concrete example of the principle in action.
- Mission f2d9f09f ('Building full-text search for AGenC missions and sessions') — notes 'thin-CLI architecture alignment', another instance.

Use `agenc session print <session-id>` on the most relevant session of mission 2703959f for the original framing.

title: Enforce cron overlap policy, timeout, and max concurrent at runtime
description: GetOverlapPolicy(), GetTimeout(), GetCronsMaxConcurrent(), and IsCronDue() are defined in config but never called in the runtime. Users can configure overlap:skip, timeout:1h, and cronsMaxConcurrent but none have any effect. Before spawning a cron mission, the server must: (1) check if a mission with the same source-id is already running and skip if overlap policy is 'skip', (2) pass the timeout to the mission/wrapper and enforce it, (3) check running cron mission count against cronsMaxConcurrent before spawning. IsCronDue is dead code and should be removed.
close_reason: Removed dead config fields (timeout, overlap, cronsMaxConcurrent) and all associated types, validators, accessors, CLI flags, and tests. These can be re-added when runtime enforcement is built.

title: Use cron UUID for plist filenames instead of sanitized name
description: CronToPlistFilename sanitizes cron names (spaces→hyphens, strip specials) but removeUnmatchedPlists does raw reverse lookup — mismatch causes orphan detection to delete+recreate plists every sync. Also enables name collisions. Fix: use cron UUID for plist filename (agenc-cron.UUID.plist) which is guaranteed unique and reversible.
close_reason: Switched plist filenames from sanitized cron name to UUID-based (agenc-cron.UUID.plist). Eliminates name collision and reverse-lookup mismatch. Legacy agenc-cron-* plists are auto-cleaned.

title: Verify plist XML output is structurally valid
description: dictFields.Entries []interface{} has no custom MarshalXML — encoding/xml might wrap entries in <Entries> tags producing invalid plist XML. Add a test that validates generated XML with plutil -lint or structural parsing.
close_reason: XML output verified valid with plutil -lint. Added permanent validation test.

title: Design progressive/layered onboarding flow
description:
Both Omar (2026-03-10) and Charles McKay (2026-03-12) hit cognitive saturation during their first AgenC exposure. Charles explicitly asked Kevin to slow down twice. Omar got lost in technical friction. The onboarding needs progressive disclosure with distinct layers: (1) Hook - one impressive capability for immediate value, (2) Claude Code basics, (3) tmux orientation, (4) first guided mission, (5) config refinement loop, (6) advanced features (multi-agent, MCP, permissions). Never show everything at once. See strategy/conversations-with-others/ for full analyses.

Additional signal from Charles McKay conversation (2026-03-12):
- Autonomy gap is quantifiable: Charles's agent runs last minutes; Kevin's longest was 7 hours (standard ~10 min). The onboarding should include an explicit "autonomy progression" — start users with short, supervised runs and gradually extend.
- 60% of engineers Kevin talks to aren't even using skills. The onboarding should assume technical users (know what an LLM is) but NOT assume Claude or agentic coding experience. Include a "Claude tips & tricks" layer and a "skills 101" layer before anything AgenC-specific — this builds the prerequisite mental model AND provides space to teach Claude patterns that make everything downstream better.

title: Resolve the confusing claude-config / shadow repo / reconfig situation
description:
The current claude-config setup is deeply confusing for new users. Multiple issues compound:

1. Agents try to edit files in $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (the AgenC-managed snapshot), which AgenC correctly blocks — but agents don't understand WHY or where they should edit instead (the source config repo).
2. 'Reconfig & reload' is the mechanism to propagate changes from the source repo to the mission's snapshot, but it's not intuitive and prints garbage output.
3. There is no first-class onboarding flow for creating a Claude config repo. Users must manually: create a repo, add CLAUDE.md/skills/commands/agents dirs, create an init.sh symlink script, push to GitHub, register with 'agenc repo add --always-synced', and configure AgenC to use it. This should be a single guided command.
4. The relationship between the config repo, the AgenC repo library copy, the per-mission claude-config snapshot, and Claude's own shadow repo at ~/.claude/projects/ is not documented anywhere.

The fix should include:
- A first-class 'agenc config init-repo' (or similar) command that creates, version-controls, pushes, and registers a Claude config repo with --always-synced in one flow
- Better AgenC-injected context so agents understand they must modify the config repo source, not $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR
- Consider making the Claude config repo a first-class AgenC concept with its own palette command for quick access
- Documentation explaining the full config lifecycle

title: Make PATH changes propagate with 'agenc server restart' — no tmux kill needed
description:
During Omar's onboarding (journal: omar-agenc-user-onboarding~2026-03-10), Claude was not on PATH inside tmux because the tmux server had been started before Claude was installed. Fixing this required exiting both the agenc server AND the tmux server, which is extremely painful and confusing.

The root cause: tmux servers inherit the shell environment at launch time. Any PATH changes made after the tmux server starts (e.g. installing Claude via brew) are invisible to all sessions within that server. The only current fix is to kill the entire tmux server and restart it, which tears down all sessions.

This is a P1 because:
1. tmux is already a leap of faith for users who've never used it before
2. Having to understand tmux server environment inheritance to fix a PATH issue is an absurd ask for a new user
3. This will hit EVERY user who installs Claude after first launching AgenC (a common sequence)

The fix should ensure that 'agenc server restart' is sufficient to pick up PATH changes. Possible approaches: have the agenc server re-source the user's shell profile when restarting, or have the wrapper script always source the profile before launching Claude, or detect and warn when Claude is not on PATH.

title: Make 'agenc config init' idempotent — retry failed steps on rerun
description: During Omar's onboarding (journal: omar-agenc-user-onboarding~2026-03-10), the OAuth token setup failed partway through 'agenc config init'. When rerun, the command just printed 'token: not set up' instead of retrying the failed step. This is a terrible experience for new users who are already in unfamiliar territory. The command must be fully idempotent: rerunning it should detect what succeeded, skip those steps, and retry what failed. Each step should be independently recoverable.

Fix 'agenc mission print' failing with 'no current session found' for missions that have sessions
description: When running 'agenc mission print $UUID', the command fails with 'no current session found for mission $UUID' even for missions that have active sessions. The bug is in cmd/mission_print.go:107. The issue is likely in how GetLastSessionID() resolves the session — it reads lastSessionId from .claude.json but may not find a match. To debug: 1) Use mission UUID 2571d5d8-ec53-47d3-b7ce-6c456eaf34db (this mission) for testing since it has 5 known sessions. 2) Read internal/claudeconfig/build.go GetLastSessionID() to understand the resolution logic. 3) Check what the .claude.json file actually contains for a failing mission. 4) Compare with what mission inspect shows (which works correctly). 5) The root cause may be that GetLastSessionID checks projectDirectoryExists() which transforms the agent dirpath using a specific algorithm — this transform may not match the actual project directory name. 6) Build with 'make build' (requires dangerouslyDisableSandbox:true), test with './agenc mission print 2571d5d8'.

title: Fix race condition in cron scheduler
description:
Fix runningCount race condition in cron_scheduler.go where count is read under lock but used outside lock.

Current (buggy) code at lines 92-94, 123-125:
s.mu.Lock()
runningCount := len(s.runningMissions)
s.mu.Unlock()
if runningCount >= maxConcurrent { continue }

Fixed version:
s.mu.Lock()
if len(s.runningMissions) >= maxConcurrent {
    s.mu.Unlock()
    continue
}
// spawn job while holding lock
s.runningMissions[missionID] = struct{}{}
s.mu.Unlock()

After fix: go test -race ./internal/daemon/... should pass

Reference: P1-7 in specs/codebase-cleanup.md
Impact: Prevents exceeding maxConcurrent limit
Estimated time: 1 hour

- Consider hybrid lexical + semantic ranking.

Out of scope for this issue: deciding the final approach. This is a research/spike + design task before implementation.

title: Add standardized field types with built-in parsing (RepoRef, Path, etc.)
description:
## Context

Several CLI commands take the same kinds of arguments — repo references, absolute paths, repo names — but each command has to remember the right parsing function. We just hit this with `agenc repo writeable-copy set`: the original code called `config.IsCanonicalRepoName`, which rejected shorthand `owner/repo` even though `agenc repo add` accepts it via `mission.ParseRepoReference`. The two commands diverged silently because there's no type-level enforcement that all repo args go through the same parser.

This is the same problem Kurtosis solved with its standardized field-type system: each domain field (PortSpec, ServiceID, FilesArtifactID, etc.) had a single type with a single canonical parser, and any code accepting that field had to use the type, which in turn forced every input through the standard parser.

## Proposal

Define standardized field types in a shared package (e.g. `internal/fields`) for the recurring argument shapes. Each type wraps a parsed value plus its original input and exposes:

- `Parse(input string) (FieldType, error)` — single canonical entry point
- Zero direct construction (private constructor + `Parse` factory) so callers cannot bypass parsing
- Display methods for round-tripping back to user-facing form

Initial candidates from existing code:

- **RepoRef** — wraps the canonical `github.com/owner/repo` name plus the originally-typed form. Backed by `mission.ParseRepoReference`. Replaces the ad-hoc `IsCanonicalRepoName` checks scattered through CLI commands.
- **AbsolutePath** — absolute, symlink-resolved, validated path. Replaces the manual expand-tilde + filepath.Abs + sanity-check sequence repeated in `config.ValidateWriteableCopyPath`, sleep-window-related code, and others.
- **MissionID** / **SessionID** / **NotificationID** — wraps a full UUID. Knows how to resolve from a short prefix via the appropriate DB lookup. Replaces the scattered short-ID logic.
- **CronSchedule** — wraps a validated 5- or 6-field cron expression.

## Why this matters

We just shipped a bug where one new CLI command rejected a perfectly valid shorthand only because the dev (me) used the wrong parser. With type-enforced fields, that mistake becomes impossible: `runRepoWriteableCopySet` would take a `fields.RepoRef` parameter and Cobra's RunE wrapper would do the parsing once, in one place. Future commands inherit the standard.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `internal/fields` package exists with at least RepoRef, AbsolutePath, and one ID type
- [ ] At least two existing CLI commands migrated to use the new fields, demonstrating the pattern
- [ ] Each field has a focused unit test covering valid + invalid inputs
- [ ] A README or doc in the package explains how to add a new field

## References

- Kurtosis design: ask Kevin for context (he ran into this in 2020-2024)
- Currently divergent: `cmd/repo_writeable_copy_set.go` (now uses ParseRepoReference) vs. the ad-hoc `IsCanonicalRepoName` checks elsewhere
- Path validation duplication: compare `internal/config/writeable_copy_path.go` to other path-handling code

title: Include 'this prompt comes from mission $UUID' header when missions spawn missions
Per-mission scoping for the AgenC unix socket — replace flat trust boundary with scoped API tokens
description:
# Per-mission scoping for the AgenC unix socket

## The problem

`~/.agenc/server/server.sock` is currently a **flat trust boundary**. Any mission with access to the socket can call any AgenC API on any other mission. In a Claude Code mission whose sandbox allows `Write(~/.agenc/server/server.sock)` (the default for missions today), that translates into:

- `agenc mission new <any-repo> --prompt '...'` — spawn arbitrary new missions in any repo the user has registered.
- `agenc mission send-keys <other-mission-id> "..."` — type into any other live mission's tmux pane. RCE-equivalent against other agents.
- `agenc mission print <other-mission-id>` — dump the full transcript of any other mission. High-value exfil channel because users concentrate sensitive content in dedicated missions (coaching journals, personal journal work, secrets discussed with specialized agents).
- `agenc session print <other-session-id>` — same, at session granularity.
- `agenc mission inspect <other-mission-id>` — discover other missions and their state.

Combined with Claude Code's `defaultMode: auto` (commands not in deny/ask are auto-allowed if sandboxable), and combined with the broader github.com / `*.vercel.com` / `*.posthog.com` exfil channels already in the network allowlist, a prompt-injected or compromised-dependency-laced agent in mission A can read mission B's transcript and exfil the contents to attacker infrastructure without ever prompting the user.

The current settings.json mitigation — moving `agenc mission new`, `mission send-keys`, `mission print`, `session print` to `ask` — closes the silent-action path but keeps the underlying API surface flat. Every cross-mission action becomes a prompt, which is the wrong primitive: the user shouldn't be approving cross-mission reads/writes one at a time, they should be configuring scopes once.

## Proposed direction

Replace the flat unix socket with a **per-mission scoped API surface**. Sketch:

1. When AgenC spawns a mission, it issues that mission an API token (or unix-socket-credential equivalent) bound to:
   - The mission's own UUID.
   - A scope set defaulting to "self only": the mission can read its own state, print its own sessions, and that's it.
2. Operations against other missions (`mission new`, `mission send-keys <other>`, `mission print <other>`, `session print <other>`, `mission inspect <other>`) require an explicitly broader scope.
3. Orchestrator-style missions (the user's "spawn another mission to do X" pattern) opt into the broader scope at spawn time — `agenc mission new --scope=orchestrator` or similar — and that gets surfaced clearly to the user when they launch one.
4. The existing CLAUDE.md guidance ("you can spawn missions to delegate work in other repos") still works for orchestrator missions; everything else gets the safer default.

This isn't a sandbox-permission problem and Claude Code can't fix it. The unix socket is one entry point; the right place to enforce mission identity and scope is on the AgenC server side.

## Why this matters now

Two recent threads pushed this up the priority list:
1. Sandbox conversation around `enableWeakerNetworkIsolation` in Claude Code — concluded that the trustd OCSP exfil channel is *much* narrower than channels the user has already accepted, including the AgenC socket. Flagging the bigger gap is the higher-leverage move.
2. `~/.claude/settings.json` was just tightened (`agenc mission new`, `mission print`, `session print`, `mission send-keys` → `ask`) as a stopgap. That works for one user with attentive prompts, but the user has been wanting per-mission scoping for a while and the ask-prompt UX won't scale.

## Out of scope for this bead

- Stronger network isolation (Anthropic's job — Claude Code TLS-terminating proxy).
- Sandbox profile changes (Claude Code's job).
- Specific token format / crypto choices — those belong in a follow-up design bead once direction is agreed.

## Provenance

Designed in AgenC mission `8d3afa33-5bd2-4cf4-9dbc-cd9a28df7e79`, session `0982938e`. Run `agenc session print 0982938e` for the full sandbox-security thread that produced this.

title: bd dolt stop falsely reports server not running in shared-server mode
description:
When run from a repo configured for shared-server mode (`dolt: shared-server: true` in .beads/config.yaml), `bd dolt stop` fails with "Error: dolt server is not running" — even when the shared server is actually running.

## Reproduction

1. From a repo with shared-server mode enabled (e.g. agent-factory, where `.beads/config.yaml` has `dolt: shared-server: true`), confirm the shared server is up:
   ```
   lsof -i :3308
   # => dolt PID listening on port 3308
   ```
2. Run:
   ```
   bd dolt stop
   ```
3. Observe: `Error: dolt server is not running` (exit 1), even though the shared dolt process is alive and bd commands like `bd list` work fine against it.

## Expected

`bd dolt stop` should detect that this repo uses the shared server (`~/.beads/shared-server/`), find the running shared dolt process (port 3308), and stop it.

## Likely cause

The `stop` command is probably looking for a *per-repo* dolt process (e.g. via a PID file in the local `.beads/`) and failing to fall through to the shared-server detection path that other commands like `bd list` and `bd dolt show` use.

## Impact

Blocks the standard rename-DB workflow from inside a repo. Forces users to either kill the shared server PID directly, or run the stop command from a different working directory where shared-server detection works.

## Environment

- bd version: 0.61.0 (from local_metadata)
- macOS, shared server at `~/.beads/shared-server/` on port 3308
- Repo: agent-factory (`.beads/config.yaml` shared-server: true)

title: Support no-Git-repo missions with filesystem directory as agent workspace
description: Enable AgenC missions that target a plain filesystem directory rather than a Git repository. This supports GitHub-oriented workflows (e.g., issue triage, PR review, project management) where the agent doesn't need a repo clone — just a working directory on the filesystem. The mission's agent directory would point to an existing directory instead of cloning a repo.

title: Sleep mode: block mission and cron creation during configured sleep windows
description: Add a sleep mode feature to AgenC that blocks non-cron mission creation and cron creation during configured time windows. Encourages the user to stop working and go to sleep. Config in config.yml with day-of-week and HH:MM time windows (matching YappBlocker's schedule format). Server-side sleepGuard middleware returns 403 with friendly message. Cron-triggered missions are exempt. No escape hatch.

title: Refactor cron CLI commands to use server CRUD endpoints
description:
Currently all cron mutation commands (config cron add, config cron update, config cron rm, cron enable, cron disable) write directly to config.yml from the CLI. Refactor these to go through server HTTP endpoints so that all mutations flow through the server.

Endpoints to add: POST /crons (create), PATCH /crons/{name} (update, enable, disable), DELETE /crons/{name} (remove). Server handlers should acquire the config lock, read/modify/write config.yml, and update cachedConfig — follow the same pattern as the sleep window endpoints in internal/server/handle_sleep.go. CLI commands become thin HTTP clients calling serverClient().

Read-only commands (cron ls, cron logs, cron history) already go through the server or read config directly — migrate these too if they read config directly.

After this lands, wrap POST /crons with the sleepGuard middleware (tracked as agenc-4ebm).

title: Teach all AgenC missions about repo library read access
description: Update the AgenC CLAUDE.md (or per-mission instructions) to document that all missions have read access to the repo library at ~/.agenc/repos/. Explain what the repo library is (managed collection of registered git repos), how to list available repos (agenc repo ls), and how to read files from them using Read/Glob/Grep tools directly against repo library paths — without needing to spawn a new mission.

title: Teach Adjutant to file beads for arbitrary repos
description: Give the Adjutant agent access to beads (bd CLI) and teach it how to create/manage beads in any repo by using BEADS_DIR pointed at the repo library. This enables the Adjutant to file issues against any registered repo without needing to be inside that repo.

title: Opus 1M context window option missing in AgenC missions
description: When running /model in a regular Claude Code session (e.g. in /tmp), the Opus 1M option is available. But in AgenC missions, /model only shows Haiku, Sonnet, and Opus — no 1M variant. Need to investigate what AgenC configuration is preventing the extended context option from appearing.
close_reason: Removed defaultModel: opus from AgenC config.yml — it was passing --model opus to Claude Code, overriding the opus[1m] setting in settings.json and hiding the 1M context option from /model

title: Add E2E test suite using agenc-test
description: Create an automated test suite that exercises the actual binary via the test-env. A TestE2E test or make e2e target that boots the test-env, runs common workflows (mission ls, repo ls, server start/stop), and verifies output. The test-env infrastructure exists but has no automated suite exercising it. Catches integration-level regressions that unit tests miss.

title: Add go mod tidy check to make check
description: Add 'go mod tidy' followed by 'git diff --exit-code go.mod go.sum' to make check. Agents sometimes add dependencies without tidying, or remove code without removing the dependency. This catches stale module state structurally.

title: Add deadcode analysis to make check
description: Add golang.org/x/tools/cmd/deadcode to the make check pipeline. The unused linter catches unexported dead code, but deadcode uses whole-program analysis to catch exported-but-unreachable code too. Agents frequently add functions 'for later' and never use them.

title: Add architecture doc staleness check to pre-commit
description: Add a pre-commit check that detects new/removed/renamed packages under internal/ and verifies docs/system-architecture.md was also modified in the same commit. Currently enforced only by convention in CLAUDE.md, which agents ignore under pressure.

title: Pin golangci-lint version in CI
description: CI currently installs golangci-lint@latest. A new lint release could silently change what passes. Pin to a specific version (e.g., v1.64.1) in .github/workflows/ci.yml and update deliberately.

title: Add govulncheck to make check
description: Add govulncheck to the make check pipeline to catch known CVEs in dependencies. Agents use latest versions by convention but don't verify vulnerabilities. One line in the Makefile catches known issues structurally.

title: Add test coverage threshold to make check
description: Add go test -coverprofile and a script/check that fails below a per-package coverage threshold (start at 60%, ratchet up over time). Currently an agent can add a new package with zero tests and the build passes. This structurally forces agents to write tests.

title: Add -race flag to go test in Makefile
description: Add the -race flag to the go test invocation in make check. This catches data races in concurrent code (server goroutines, tmux, file watchers) that agents routinely introduce without realizing.

title: Unify cron next-run calculation to a single code path
description: GetNextCronRun (agenc_config.go:1075) uses the gronx library while actual scheduling uses the hand-rolled ParseCronExpression -> launchd StartCalendarInterval path. These two parsers could disagree on edge cases. Consolidate to a single code path so display and scheduling always agree. Either derive next-run from the CalendarInterval that launchd actually uses, or validate that gronx and ParseCronExpression produce equivalent results for all accepted expressions.
close_reason: Removed GetNextCronRun, NextRunFromCalendarInterval, and all next-run display code from cron ls/new/enable/config-cron-add. Removed gronx dependency.

title: Add file locking to config.yml read-modify-write operations
description: CLI commands (enable, disable, add, rm, update) all perform read-modify-write on config.yml without file locking. Two concurrent CLI invocations can race and one write is lost. Solution: add flock-based file locking around config read-modify-write cycles. This is simpler than routing all mutations through the server and works without the server running.

title: Add fallback recovery when unload fails during cron plist reload
description: In cron_syncer.go syncCronJob, when content changes and the job is loaded, UnloadPlist failure is logged but execution continues to LoadPlist. If the job is still loaded (because unload failed), launchctl load will reject the duplicate, and the error IS returned — leaving the cron broken with new plist on disk but old schedule in launchd. Fix: if UnloadPlist fails, try RemoveJobByLabel as a fallback before attempting LoadPlist.

title: Add missing cron syncer test coverage
description: Missing tests for: disabled crons (unload path), no-ID skip path, error paths (LoadPlist/UnloadPlist failures), removeUnmatchedPlists/orphan removal, concurrent sync safety, plist with no StartCalendarInterval, Sunday normalization (weekday 7→0), sanitized name collisions, RemovePlist, ListAgencCronJobs parsing.
close_reason: Added tests for disabled crons (unload path + not-loaded noop), launchd orphan detection (removes orphan UUIDs and legacy labels, preserves known). Also added plutil validation test and ptrToString fix in earlier commits.

title: Add sync.Mutex to CronSyncer to prevent concurrent syncs
description: SyncCronsToLaunchd is called from startup (sync) and fsnotify callbacks (goroutine). Two rapid config changes >500ms apart can produce concurrent syncs racing on the same plist files and launchctl calls.
close_reason: Added sync.Mutex to CronSyncer, locked at top of SyncCronsToLaunchd

title: Add launchd-side orphan detection to cron syncer
description: reconcileOrphanedPlists only scans filesystem for plist files. If a plist is deleted while the job is loaded in launchd, the phantom job persists forever. Use ListAgencCronJobs + RemoveJobByLabel to catch loaded jobs with no corresponding config entry.
close_reason: Added removeOrphanedLaunchdJobs that calls ListAgencCronJobs and RemoveJobByLabel to catch phantom loaded jobs with no config entry

title: Use AGENC_DIRPATH-based prefix for generated plist files to isolate test environments
description:
When generating launchd plist files, use a prefix derived from AGENC_DIRPATH to prevent collisions between the main AgenC installation and per-agent test environments.

**Rules:**
- If AGENC_DIRPATH is empty (default): prefix plist filenames with 'agenc.'
- If AGENC_DIRPATH is set: prefix plist filenames with 'agenc-HASH.' where HASH is the SHA hash of the absolute resolved path of AGENC_DIRPATH

This allows agents running their own pocket AgenC environment (with a custom AGENC_DIRPATH) to register launchd services without colliding with the main system's plist files or each other's.
close_reason: Plist prefix now derived dynamically from AGENC_DIRPATH via config.GetCronPlistPrefix()

title: Fix unchecked fsnotify.Watcher.Add error
description: The errcheck exclusion for fsnotify.Watcher.Add hides a bug in the addWatch() helper function in internal/server/config_watcher.go lines 192-194. This function silently ignores errors, meaning if watcher.Add() fails due to permissions, config changes won't be detected. Most other callsites (config_watcher.go:64, wrapper.go:549, credential_sync.go:158) already check the error properly. Fix: remove the addWatch() helper and check errors at the callsite, or have addWatch() return the error. Remove Watcher.Add from errcheck exclusions.
close_reason: Removed blanket Watcher.Add errcheck exclusion. addWatch() helper now uses explicit _ = with comment explaining intentional ignore during recursive walks. All other callsites already check errors.

title: Fix unchecked exec.Cmd.Run/Wait and os.Process.Kill errors
description: The errcheck exclusions for exec.Cmd.Run, exec.Cmd.Wait, and os.Process.Kill are too broad. Exit codes matter in several contexts: tmux state changes (wrapper/tmux.go), process lifecycle (server/pool.go). Some callsites already check the error despite the exclusion being active. Remove these from errcheck exclusions and add proper error handling where exit codes matter. For genuinely fire-and-forget contexts (e.g., best-effort tmux display updates), use explicit _ = assignment.
close_reason: Removed exec.Cmd.Run, exec.Cmd.Wait, os.Process.Kill from errcheck exclusions. All 11 flagged instances were genuinely fire-and-forget (tmux cleanup, test teardown) — added explicit _ = assignments.

title: Fix unchecked os.Rename errors in log rotation
description: The errcheck exclusion for os.Rename hides silent log rotation failures. In internal/wrapper/wrapper.go lines 844 and 848, os.Rename is used for log rotation without error checking. If rotation fails, logs never rotate and disk usage grows unbounded. Remove os.Rename from errcheck exclusions and log rotation failures at the callsite (don't need to return error — just log it).
close_reason: Added error logging to log rotation os.Rename calls in wrapper.go. Removed blanket os.Rename errcheck exclusion. All other callsites already checked errors.

title: Fix unchecked os.Remove/os.RemoveAll errors
description: The errcheck exclusions for os.Remove and os.RemoveAll are hiding real bugs. Critical instances: (1) wrapper/socket.go lines 65, 80, 99 — if socket file removal fails, subsequent Listen() fails with 'address in use'. (2) claudeconfig/shadow.go line 379 — os.RemoveAll in loop silently fails on permission errors. (3) server/server.go lines 127, 199 — socket removal failure causes startup failure. Remove these from errcheck exclusions and add proper error handling at each callsite. Defer-cleanup contexts (cmd/mission_draft.go, test files) are acceptable — use explicit _ = assignment for those.
close_reason: Fixed critical socket removal errors (wrapper/socket.go, server/server.go), added error logging in shadow.go loop, added explicit _ = for acceptable cleanup patterns. Removed os.Remove and os.RemoveAll from errcheck exclusions.

title: Lower cyclomatic complexity threshold from 35 to 20
description: The golangci-lint config was set to complexity 35, which effectively disables the check. The original was 15. Set to 20 as a pragmatic starting point — this catches genuinely problematic functions while allowing the handful with essential complexity (concurrent state management in stash handlers, wrapper event loops). Tighten further after refactoring the worst offenders.
close_reason: Lowered min-complexity from 35 to 20. Refactored all 10 remaining functions above threshold: extracted helpers from runCronNew, runFzfPickerCore, promptForTitle, printDailySummary, MergeSettingsWithAgencOverrides, ingestDir, scanJSONLFromOffset; table-driven migrations in database.Open; helper extraction in test functions.

title: Integrate doc regeneration into .githooks/pre-commit
description: scripts/hooks/pre-commit auto-regenerates CLI docs when cmd/ files change, but .githooks/pre-commit (the actual git hook) doesn't call it. The doc regeneration logic should be moved into .githooks/pre-commit so it actually runs on commit.

title: Integrate golangci-lint into Makefile check target
description:
The repo has a .golangci.yml config but golangci-lint is not actually run anywhere. Integrate it into the build pipeline following the pattern from mieubrisse/go-cli-template:

1. Add 'golangci-lint run ./...' to the Makefile 'check' target, between 'go vet' and tests
2. Add a GitHub Actions CI workflow (.github/workflows/ci.yml) that installs golangci-lint and runs 'make check'

Reference: go-cli-template Makefile runs golangci-lint in check target, and ci.yml installs it via 'go install github.com/golangci/golangci-lint/cmd/golangci-lint@latest'.

title: Initialize sane tmux defaults when no .tmux.conf exists
description: AgenC relies heavily on tmux but doesn't ensure sane defaults are in place. If the user has no .tmux.conf, tmux runs with its (often surprising) defaults. AgenC should detect the absence of .tmux.conf and set sane defaults programmatically (e.g. via tmux set-option) to ensure a consistent experience.

title: Give Side Draft the ability to paste in Claude's last response for user markup
description: Add a palette command or hotkey in Side Draft that pastes Claude's most recent response into the draft editor. This lets the user mark up, annotate, or edit the response before sending it back — enabling a tighter feedback loop where the user can visually indicate what to change rather than describing it in text.

title: Eliminate package-level agencDirpath global in cmd package
description: The cmd package relies on a package-level 'agencDirpath' variable that gets magically set as a side effect of getAgencContext(). Different code paths may or may not call getAgencContext(), leading to bugs where agencDirpath is empty (e.g., the --run-wrapper code path in doRunWrapperDirect). The fix that papered over this just added another magic initializer. The proper fix is to refactor the cmd package so agencDirpath is resolved once at the entry point of each command and passed explicitly through the call chain as a function parameter, eliminating the global.
close_reason: Eliminated package-level agencDirpath global. 24 files changed: removed global from root.go, deleted getAgencContext(), made readConfig/readConfigWithComments/ensureServerRunning resolve path internally, all cmd/ callers use config.GetAgencDirpath() at point of use.

title: Verify detaching mission window cleans up side shell pane from agenc-pool
description: When a user detaches a mission window while a side shell pane is open, the side shell pane should not persist in the agenc-pool tmux session. Need to verify this behavior and fix if broken.
close_reason: Added killExtraPanesInWindow() to pool.go, called from handleDetachMission after unlink. On detach, any panes in the pool window that aren't the registered wrapper pane are killed.

title: Fix ESC not escaping from new Repo screen
description: Pressing ESC on the 'new Repo' screen does not escape/cancel as expected. ESC should dismiss the screen and return to the previous view.

title: Fix PostToolUse/PostToolUseFailure tab state using StopFailure hook
description: After a permission request, the tmux window title/color should return to the 'busy' (blue) state. Currently PostToolUse and PostToolUseFailure don't properly reset Claude's state. Should hook into the new StopFailure hook to handle this correctly.

title: Self-contained test environment for agent e2e validation
description:
Allow agents working on the AgenC codebase to build, run, and test their own changes in a fully isolated pocket environment. See docs/plans/2026-03-24-test-env-design.md for the full design.

Key components:
1. Namespace resolver — system-global resources (tmux sessions, launchd plists) get a hash suffix derived from AGENC_DIRPATH
2. Wrapper script — _build/agenc-test sets AGENC_DIRPATH and AGENC_TEST_ENV=1, execs _build/agenc
3. make test-env / make test-env-clean — create and tear down the pocket environment at _test-env/
4. AGENC_TEST_ENV gating — features that conflict with global state (tmux keybinding injection) return errors in test environments
5. _build/ for binaries, _test-env/ for runtime state — independent lifecycles
close_reason: Implemented namespace isolation (tmux sessions, launchd plists), AGENC_TEST_ENV gating, wrapper script, make targets, and CLAUDE.md docs

title: Give agents explicit instructions about repo library at $AGENC_DIRPATH/repos
description: Ensure agents have clear instructions (if they don't already) that the user's cloned repos live at $AGENC_DIRPATH/repos and agents can explore them as needed for reference. Check whether the prime content or CLAUDE.md already covers this; if not, add it.

title: Explore Supergateway for system-level MCP server caching
description: Explore using Supergateway so that MCP servers don't need to be restarted upon each mission that uses them, and MCP servers instead get cached at the system level. This also addresses the persistent auth problem: MCP servers could be instantiated once at a system level, auth'd via 1Password secrets, and each mission just calls into the shared MCP server. The user would only need to auth to 1Password once, rather than re-authing for every mission.

title: Adjutant-assisted work triage and prioritization
description:
Once AgenC has a work tracking system (agenc-181j), the Adjutant should help users triage and prioritize incoming work. The model is a 'work hopper' — tasks flow in from ambient capture (Siri voice notes, email, chat, agent-generated items) and accumulate in an inbox. The user then decides what gets 'let into the factory' (i.e., becomes a mission). The Adjutant's role: surface what's in the inbox, help the user prioritize based on urgency/impact/dependencies, suggest which items to greenlight into missions, and flag items that have been sitting too long. Omar's Siri inbox → Claude triage pipeline is an early example of this pattern. This is the 'CEO of an agent org' vision applied to work intake — the user makes strategic decisions about what to work on, agents handle execution.

Key concept: MIRN (Most Important Right Now). The Adjutant should help users identify from their global task list which tasks are the biggest levers — the most important ones to work on right now. The global task view is a necessary input to output a MIRN-prioritized view. This is linked to the /prioritization skill's MIRN concept.

[UPDATE 2026-03-18] May (non-technical PMM user, March 6 interview) independently validated this need from outside the technical ICP: 'I need to keep surfacing things to ask, is this worth prioritizing right now?' and 'I don't think all the things I'm doing right now are the RIGHT things to get me where I want to go.' Her core unmet need is prioritization, not execution. She wants to build a personal assistant ('April') that captures thoughts, categorizes them P0-P3, and sends reminders. This confirms the triage/prioritization need exists across both technical and non-technical users. Signal strength upgraded from SINGLE-SIGNAL to VALIDATED.
[Source]: strategy/conversations-with-others/2026-03-06_may-gong-user-interview.md

Omar didn't know what compaction was or whether he should restart sessions. This is a common confusion for new Claude users.

Omar's biggest pain with side missions was losing track of whether they'd pushed to Git — this drove him back to single-mission workflow. Currently auto-commit/push is configured in CLAUDE.md but relies on agent compliance. This should be an opt-in setting per repo, not a global enforcement.

title: Show 'what's next' summary in mission switcher

Currently users see mission descriptions but not what state the mission is in or what it needs. Omar explicitly requested an 'inbox view' where you see which missions need attention. This builds on agenc-321 (AI summarization) — the summary could be generated by the summarization daemon and displayed in the picker.

title: Pluggable task management interface (agenc task)
description: Expose an 'agenc task' interface that supports different backend implementations: Obsidian/Markdown (Omar), Markdown repo (Yannik), Todoist (Kevin). This is a potential product differentiator — baking GTD methodology into AgenC while letting users keep their preferred task storage. Key challenges: no easy dependency graph or queries with pure Markdown. Consider a background sync daemon that monitors Markdown files, keeps a DB for queries, injects UUIDs, and builds dependency graphs. Three users have independently expressed the need for structured task management inside agent workflows.
notes: [2026-03-18] The question of what the GLOBAL task tracker will be inside AgenC remains open. The pluggable interface is the architecture, but the question of which system AgenC itself uses as its canonical global task view is unresolved.

title: Green tab color when Claude is done, clears after user views
description: Show dark green on tmux tabs when Claude has finished and is waiting for input. The color should revert to default after the user has looked at the tab for a moment. Both Omar and Yannik requested this. Requires building a polling loop to detect when the user is viewing a specific tmux pane, as tmux has no native hook for this. Orange remains reserved for permission requests.

title: Auto-capture session results to persistent storage

notes: Related: agenc-3ms2 (auto-retrospective). Both are session-close automation under the CEO vision (agenc-uui).

title: Auto-retrospective on session close
description: When a Claude session closes (Ctrl+C, idle-kill, or explicit stop), automatically run a retrospective that analyzes the session for friction points and suggests improvements to CLAUDE.md, skills, or settings. Omar built /retrospective manually and explicitly requested this be automated. Related to agenc-317 (monitoring) and agenc-321 (summarization) but focused specifically on the close-time improvement extraction, not summarization.
notes: Related: agenc-1tz0 (auto-capture results). Both are session-close automation under the CEO vision (agenc-uui).

title: Build skill sharing/distribution mechanism via Git
description: Build a mechanism for sharing and discovering skills via Git repositories. Users independently build skills that mirror each other's work — a sharing mechanism would eliminate redundant effort and create network effects.

title: Run missions in Docker containers for safe YOLO mode
description: Running missions inside Docker containers would allow full YOLO mode (unrestricted agent permissions) without risk to the host system. This eliminates the need for complex allow/deny permission lists and makes the trust model simpler: the container IS the sandbox.

title: Consider adding default allowed commands to settings.json for fresh Claude onboarding
description: New Claude users are bombarded with permission prompts for basic commands (git add, ls, touch, file, mkdir, etc.). Consider adding a set of safe default allowed commands to the AgenC-generated settings.json to reduce friction for people who are fresh-onboarding to both AgenC and Claude. This could be an opinionated default that users can override.

title: Add 'Claude Explained' documentation section for new Claude users
description:
Many AgenC users will be new to Claude Code. Add a documentation section covering:
- How Claude sessions work (context window, conversation history, session persistence)
- The settings.json permission prompt: what it means, how it works, how to configure it (link to agenc-tcoh for the claude-config situation)
- When to use CLAUDE.md vs skills: global CLAUDE.md (every mission gets this context, always), global skills (every mission has the option, loaded on demand), repo CLAUDE.md (every mission to that repo gets the context, always), repo skills (every mission to that repo has the option, loaded on demand). Omar was confused about this distinction.
- What the 'don't ask again' Claude permission prompt means in an AgenC context: it means the MISSION won't ask again, because settings.local.json is mission-specific and ephemeral

title: Research and implement a documentation site tool (Docusaurus, alternatives)

title: Create a documentation website for AgenC
description: AgenC needs a dedicated documentation website beyond the README. The README is getting long and can't serve as both marketing landing page and comprehensive docs. A proper docs site would improve discoverability, allow structured navigation, and make AgenC more professional and approachable for new users.

title: Research requirements for building a native Mac app (a la Conductor)
description: Research what would be needed to create a Mac app for AgenC, similar to the Conductor app. Evaluate: Swift/AppKit vs Electron vs Tauri, terminal emulation libraries, tmux integration vs replacement, distribution (App Store vs direct download vs Homebrew cask), code signing requirements, and estimated engineering effort.

title: Build a GUI for AgenC
description:
tmux is the single biggest barrier to entry for new users. It adds unfamiliar hotkeys, a confusing server/session model, opaque status bars, and environment inheritance issues (see agenc-2ome for the PATH problem). Omar's onboarding session confirmed this — he'd never used screen/tmux before and the learning curve was steep.

A native GUI would:
- Lower the barrier to entry dramatically (just download and run)
- Allow better visual presentation (colors, tabs, Markdown rendering)
- Enable custom input syntax (e.g. # for mission ID tab-complete, @ to summon agents)
- Eliminate the tmux environment inheritance problem entirely
- Make the product more demo-friendly and visually compelling

This is a large investment but may be necessary to reach the ICP (technical but not tmux-native users).

title: Consider building prompt-engineer skill into AgenC as auto-generated per-mission skill

AgenC could auto-generate it with each mission, with a config flag to disable it for users who don't want it. Omar found this skill immediately valuable during onboarding.

title: Teach Adjutant what 'always synced' means

The Adjutant prompt should explain that --always-synced repos are automatically kept in sync with their remote — AgenC pulls the latest version before each mission launch, so the mission always gets fresh content. This is critical for config repos that are updated frequently.

title: Make Adjutant aware of the user's GitHub username

title: Create starter .tmux.conf with recommended settings for new users

title: Create pre-commit hook for code quality
description:
Create a pre-commit hook that enforces gofmt and runs go vet.

Create .git/hooks/pre-commit:
#\!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

unformatted=$(gofmt -l .)
if [[ -n "$unformatted" ]]; then
    echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
    echo "Run: gofmt -w ."
    exit 1
fi

go vet ./...

Make executable: chmod +x .git/hooks/pre-commit
Test by attempting a commit with unformatted code

Stop Mission palette command should return instantly
description: The Stop Mission palette command currently blocks the tmux popup until the mission finishes shutting down. It should return control to the user immediately so they can navigate to other tmux panes while the stop runs in the background. Attempted fixes: (1) appending & to background the shell command — didn't work, likely because the tmux display-popup PTY teardown kills the backgrounded process. (2) Using nohup with output redirection and & — also didn't work. The root challenge is that the palette runs inside tmux display-popup -E, which destroys the PTY when the popup command exits, likely killing any child processes. A solution needs to fully detach the stop process from the popup's process group/session (e.g., setsid, or handling it in Go code with SysProcAttr.Setsid, or having the palette write to a file/socket that a daemon picks up).

title: Fix missing logging + race condition in template change reload path
description: The configChanged handler in wrapper.go:183-198 has zero logging (unlike the globalConfigChanged handler which logs at line 204). Template rsyncs and state transitions happen silently. Race condition: when Claude exits naturally, both claudeStateIdle (from the Stop hook) and claudeExited arrive on the select simultaneously. If claudeExited wins while state is StateRestartPending (not yet StateRestarting), the wrapper exits at line 181 without restarting — the pending restart is lost.
close_reason: No longer relevant: template reload path removed

title: Add 'agenc session print --until' flag for queue-based work handoffs
description:
Feature request: extend 'agenc session print' (or equivalent) with an --until flag that lets a session/mission be sliced at a specific point in time or up to a specific event/marker.

Use case: queue-based work handoff. An agent files a bead for a future agent to pick up. The future agent should be able to read EXACTLY the context the filing agent was working with — not the whole session transcript, but the slice of it leading up to the bead-filing moment. This makes inherited context faithful and bounded.

Today: future agents either get no context (just the bead description) or full transcripts (slow, often-irrelevant). An --until flag is the missing middle.

Sketch: 'agenc session print <session-id> --until <timestamp|marker|event>' returns the transcript up to that point only. Bead descriptions can reference the cutoff explicitly so consumers know what to ask for.

Captured during inbox processing on 2026-05-09.

title: Build personalized HN summary cron loop (filtered to Kevin's interests)
description:
Outcome: an AgenC cron mission that periodically (e.g., daily) scans Hacker News and produces a digest of posts Kevin would personally find interesting — filtered through his known interests, taste, and current focus areas (entrepreneurship, AI/agents, craftsmanship, myopia, distribution, storytelling, taste).

This is DIFFERENT from bead agenc-5zn1 (the forward-looking AI/tech research cron loop). Distinction:
- agenc-5zn1: 'what's coming down the pipe in tech and AI' — horizon scan, focuses on transformative innovations
- this bead: 'what would Kevin personally find interesting on HN today' — personal-interest filter, broader topic mix

Investigation hints:
- Source: HN front page, /best, /newest. Pull via the HN API (https://github.com/HackerNews/API) or RSS.
- Filter: use Kevin's profile (/about-me, /my-artistic-taste, /storytelling, current focus) to score each post for personal relevance. Likely a Claude prompt that scores 'would Kevin care about this'.
- Output: similar shape to agenc-5zn1 — a digest, possibly a journal entry, Todoist task, or notification. Coordinate with that bead to share infrastructure.

Coordinate with agenc-5zn1 (the horizon loop) to share cron pattern, output channel, and any HN-fetching code so the two loops don't duplicate infra.

title: Build forward-looking AI/tech research cron loop
description:
A scheduled AgenC cron mission that periodically (e.g., daily or weekly) does forward-looking research on what's coming down the pipe in AI and adjacent tech. Output: a summary of innovations, transformative features, and things the user should pay attention to — including ideas worth building into AgenC itself.

Outcome: a cron-managed AgenC mission that runs on schedule, performs the research, and surfaces a digest the user can review. Should highlight 'interesting' or 'pay attention' items rather than dump everything.

Companion: there is a Todoist NTW project '🔭 Build a what's-on-the-horizon tech research loop' tracking the user-side framing. This bead is the AgenC-side implementation work.

Investigation hints: check existing AgenC cron mission patterns (`agenc cron list`); decide on research source mix (RSS, web, X, hacker news, papers); decide on output channel (journal entry? notification? Todoist task?).

title: Restore last-active column in mission attach dialog
description:
The 'agenc mission attach' interactive dialog used to display a last-active timestamp column for each session. That column has gone missing — the data may still be present, but it's not rendering, OR the data is no longer being populated. Outcome: the attach dialog shows the correct last-active timestamp for every session listed.

Investigation hints:
- Search past missions for context: `agenc mission search 'last-active'` surfaces mission f2d9f09f (Building full-text search for AGenC missions and sessions) which discusses last_active as a field — that mission's work is likely where the timestamp logic lives.
- Look at the attach dialog rendering code (likely in internal/cli/mission/attach or similar) and trace where the last-active column is constructed.

Reproduce: run `agenc mission attach` and observe the absence of the last-active column. The user reports it used to be there.
close_reason: Superseded — column restored as LAST PROMPT (last_user_prompt_at) instead of LAST ACTIVE (last_heartbeat). See docs/plans/2026-05-09-last-prompt-column-design.md

title: E2E regression test for ~/.claude → mission reload auto-propagation
description: Add an E2E test in scripts/e2e-test.sh that verifies: (1) editing ~/.claude/ propagates into a running mission's claude-config/ after agenc mission reload --async fires. Two prerequisites surfaced during Task 8 of the wrapper auto-regen plan: (1) Test env's HOME is not sandboxed by _build/agenc-test, so the test must isolate ~/.claude/ writes (e.g., set HOME=mktemp under the e2e harness, or extend agenc-test to sandbox HOME), AND (2) config_commit needs CLI exposure (see follow-up beads issue) so the test can assert the value advanced. Once both prereqs are in place, the prescribed regression test in docs/plans/2026-05-06-wrapper-auto-regen-claude-config-plan.md Task 8 Step 3 can be implemented safely.

title: Expose config_commit via CLI (mission inspect or mission ls --json)
description: To enable a proper E2E regression test for the wrapper auto-regen behavior, the mission's config_commit DB column needs to be visible from the CLI. Currently neither mission ls (with or without -a) nor mission inspect surfaces it. Options: (a) add a config_commit column to mission ls -a output, (b) add a JSON output mode (mission ls -a --json) that includes all DB fields, (c) add a mission inspect --field config_commit accessor. Surfaced during Task 8 of the wrapper auto-regen plan when the prescribed E2E regression assertion 'config_commit advanced after reload' could not be implemented. Blocks: agenc-e2e-autoregen.

title: Handle stale reconfigMission palette entries in user agenc.yml
description: After Task 4 of the wrapper auto-regen plan removed the reconfigMission default palette entry (commit 0b3a0a5), users who persisted a partial override like 'reconfigMission: { tmuxKeybinding: "C-r" }' in their agenc.yml will hit config-load failure: validatePaletteCommandConfigs (internal/config/agenc_config.go:697-717) now treats reconfigMission as a custom entry missing required title and command, refusing to load. Cases that still load cleanly: full overrides (title+command set), entries with disabled=true, or configs with no reconfigMission key at all. Options: (a) add a one-time migration stripping bare reconfigMission keys, (b) warn-and-drop unknown-but-stale-builtin-shaped keys in the validator, (c) document in CHANGELOG/release notes only. Surfaced by code-quality reviewer on commit 0b3a0a5.

title: Clarify agent_instructions.md vs agenc prime vs adjutant_claude.md roles
description:
Today there are three places that inject context into an agent's Claude:

1. **`internal/claudeconfig/agent_instructions.md`** — baked into every regular mission's CLAUDE.md at config-build time.
2. **`internal/claudeconfig/prime_content.md`** (via `agenc prime`) — auto-generated from the cobra command tree; Adjutant gets it via SessionStart hook, regular missions don't get it baked in.
3. **`internal/claudeconfig/adjutant_claude.md`** — Adjutant-specific prompt with user-facing feature documentation.

The split is currently unprincipled: when adding a new piece of guidance (e.g., the recent `mission reload --async` rule for self-reloading agents), it's not obvious which file owns it. The CLAUDE.md notes some guidance for Adjutant content, but the broader model — what each file is FOR, what their relationships are, whether they should overlap, and whether all missions should see all three — has never been explicitly designed.

Investigate and produce a clear architectural decision:
- What is each file's purpose / audience?
- Should regular missions also receive prime content baked in (already raised in `agenc-oup1`)?
- Should Adjutant also receive agent_instructions, or is its content fully separate?
- When new agent-facing guidance is added, what's the routing rule?
- Are there pieces of agent_instructions that belong in prime, or vice versa?

The existing issue `agenc-oup1` covers a narrow slice (the inject asymmetry between Adjutant and regular missions). This is the broader strategic question.

title: Add Lucene-style query syntax to mission search
description: Support field:value filter syntax in mission search queries (e.g. repo:agenc, title:refactor, before:2026-04-01). Unqualified terms default to full-text search across all fields. Inspired by Gmail/GitHub search syntax. Prerequisite: multi-path search (title, repo, transcript, UUID) should be working first.

title: Move detach picker's filterLinkedMissions to server side
description: The detach picker uses getCurrentTmuxSessionName() to filter missions linked to the current session. This is inconsistent with the thin-CLI/thick-server pattern established in the CallingPaneID refactor. Move the filtering to the server: add a query parameter to ListMissions (or a new endpoint) that accepts a pane ID and returns only missions linked to that pane's session. Then remove getCurrentTmuxSessionName() from mission_detach.go.

title: Unify agent_instructions.md and prime_content.md injection
description: Currently Adjutant gets prime (via SessionStart hook) but not agent_instructions baked into CLAUDE.md, while regular missions get agent_instructions baked into CLAUDE.md but not prime. This asymmetry is odd — both should get both. Investigate and unify the injection model so all missions receive the same baseline context.

title: Auto-detect GitHub repo renames via redirect and offer to fix
description: The repo update loop already fetches repos every 60 seconds. If a fetch notices the remote URL redirects to a different canonical path (HTTP 301), AgenC could detect the rename and either auto-fix it or prompt the user. This transforms repo mv from a manual maintenance command into a self-healing system. Implementation: during ForceUpdateRepo, check if git ls-remote returns a different canonical URL than what's stored, and if so, trigger the rename flow using the existing handleMoveRepo handler.

title: Migrate E2E tests from Bash to Go
description: The E2E test script (scripts/e2e-test.sh) reimplements test infrastructure that Go's testing package provides natively: pass/fail counting, output assertions, test grouping. Migrate to Go tests using os/exec.Command to shell out to _build/agenc-test. Benefits: subtests, parallel execution, structured setup/teardown via TestMain, runs as part of make check, better failure output. The Makefile's test-env setup/teardown logic would move into TestMain.

title: Add 'mission migrate' command to move missions between tmux sessions
description: Add a 'mission migrate' command that moves a mission's tmux window from one session to another. Implementation is straightforward: run 'mission attach' in the target session and 'mission detach' from the source session. This enables reorganizing missions across sessions without manual tmux gymnastics.

title: Auto-install tmux keybindings on agenc init
description: When running 'agenc init', automatically install AgenC's tmux keybindings. This removes a manual setup step and ensures users get the full command palette experience immediately. Related to agenc-ro6i (auto-generating .tmux.conf).

title: Auto-generate .tmux.conf with sane defaults when not found
description: AgenC should detect when no .tmux.conf exists and auto-generate one with the user's preferred defaults. The specific defaults will be provided at implementation time. This ensures a consistent tmux experience across missions without requiring manual setup.

title: Replace blanket -SA1019 staticcheck exclusion with targeted nolint directives
description: The .golangci.yml staticcheck settings exclude '-SA1019' (deprecated API usage) globally, but there are only 2 instances in the entire codebase: (1) cmd/server_version_check.go:52 — config.GetDaemonPIDFilepath(agencDirpath), deprecated because daemon was replaced by server. (2) cmd/server_version_check.go:67 — config.GetDaemonDirpath(agencDirpath), same reason. Both are in server_version_check.go which INTENTIONALLY uses deprecated daemon functions — its entire purpose is to clean up old daemon artifacts during upgrade. The blanket SA1019 suppression means any new deprecated API usage anywhere in the codebase accumulates silently without warning. Steps: (1) Remove '-SA1019' from the staticcheck checks list in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' to find flagged instances. (3) At the 2 callsites in server_version_check.go, add targeted '//nolint:staticcheck // intentionally using deprecated daemon paths for migration cleanup' comments. (4) If any other SA1019 violations appear elsewhere, evaluate them: either fix the deprecated usage or add a targeted nolint with explanation. (5) Commit. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").
close_reason: Removed blanket -SA1019 exclusion, added targeted nolint:staticcheck at 2 callsites in server_version_check.go. Also removed inert os.Stdout.Write exclusion.

title: Replace blanket fmt.Sscanf errcheck exclusion with targeted _ =
description: The .golangci.yml errcheck exclude-functions list contains 'fmt.Sscanf' but there is only 1 unchecked instance in production code: internal/server/pane_reconcile.go:102 — fmt.Sscanf(parts[1], "%d", &pid). This callsite is defensive: if parsing fails, pid remains 0, and the subsequent 'if pid > 0' check on line 103 filters out invalid entries. The error is intentionally ignored with a defensive fallback. The blanket exclusion is overly broad for this one instance. Steps: (1) Remove 'fmt.Sscanf' from errcheck exclude-functions in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' to find flagged instances. (3) At the callsite in pane_reconcile.go:102, add explicit '_ =' with a comment like '// parse failure leaves pid=0, filtered by subsequent check'. (4) Fix any other newly-flagged instances with proper error handling. (5) Commit. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").
close_reason: Removed fmt.Sscanf from errcheck exclusions, added _, _ = with explanatory comment at pane_reconcile.go:102

title: Replace blanket json.Encoder.Encode errcheck exclusion with targeted _ =
description: The .golangci.yml errcheck exclude-functions list contains '(*encoding/json.Encoder).Encode' but all 4 unchecked instances in production code are HTTP handler patterns writing to http.ResponseWriter: (1) internal/wrapper/socket.go:124 — json.NewEncoder(rw).Encode(resp) in status handler. (2) internal/wrapper/socket.go:192 — same pattern in another handler. (3) internal/server/errors.go:47 — json.NewEncoder(w).Encode(data) in error response helper. (4) internal/server/middleware.go:60 — json.NewEncoder(lrw.ResponseWriter).Encode(...) in error handler. All 4 are standard HTTP handler patterns where the response has already started (headers written) and there's nothing meaningful to do with an encode error. However, the blanket exclusion could hide errors if someone uses json.Encoder to write to a file in the future. Steps: (1) Remove '(*encoding/json.Encoder).Encode' from errcheck exclude-functions in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' to find flagged instances. (3) At each HTTP handler callsite, add explicit '_ =' with a comment like '// response already started; encode error cannot be propagated'. (4) If any non-HTTP instances are flagged, those need proper error handling. (5) Commit. Note: there are also 4 instances in internal/server/client.go that use pw.CloseWithError(json.NewEncoder(pw).Encode(body)) — these already handle the error properly via CloseWithError and should not be affected. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").

title: Replace blanket os.MkdirAll errcheck exclusion with targeted fix
description: The .golangci.yml errcheck exclude-functions list contains 'os.MkdirAll' but there is only 1 unchecked instance in production code: cmd/tmux_palette.go:257 — os.MkdirAll(filepath.Dir(logFilepath), 0755). Every other os.MkdirAll callsite in the codebase already checks the error. The blanket exclusion exists solely for this one callsite and is overly broad. Steps: (1) Remove 'os.MkdirAll' from errcheck exclude-functions in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' to find flagged instances. (3) Fix cmd/tmux_palette.go:257 — either check the error and handle it (log a warning, since this is palette log directory creation and failure is non-fatal) or use explicit '_ =' with a comment explaining why. (4) Fix any other newly-flagged instances the same way. (5) Commit. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").
close_reason: Removed os.MkdirAll exclusion. Added _ = at the 1 non-fatal callsite in tmux_palette.go.

title: Replace blanket (*bufio.Reader).ReadBytes errcheck exclusion with targeted _ =
description: The .golangci.yml errcheck exclude-functions list contains '(*bufio.Reader).ReadBytes' but there are only 2 unchecked instances in production code, both identical 'press enter to continue' patterns: (1) cmd/mission_resume.go:62 — bufio.NewReader(os.Stdin).ReadBytes('\n') after error message. (2) internal/wrapper/wrapper.go:409 — same pattern after Claude exits. Both are intentionally fire-and-forget. A blanket exclusion is overly broad and could hide real read errors in future code. Steps: (1) Remove '(*bufio.Reader).ReadBytes' from errcheck exclude-functions in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' to find flagged instances. (3) At each callsite, add explicit '_ =' assignment with a comment like '// intentionally ignored: press-enter-to-continue prompt'. (4) Commit. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").
close_reason: Removed (*bufio.Reader).ReadBytes exclusion. Added explicit _ = at 2 press-enter-to-continue callsites.

title: Replace blanket (*os.File).Write errcheck exclusion with targeted suppression
description: The .golangci.yml errcheck exclude-functions list contains '(*os.File).Write' labeled as 'Close/cleanup in defer' which is incorrect — it's a write operation, not a close. All 4 unchecked instances in production code are actually os.Stdout.Write calls, which are ALREADY covered by the separate 'os.Stdout.Write' exclusion on line 30. The (*os.File).Write exclusion is both redundant and overly broad — it would silently allow ignoring write errors on actual files. Steps: (1) Remove '(*os.File).Write' from errcheck exclude-functions in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' to see if anything new is flagged. Since all current instances are os.Stdout.Write (covered by the separate exclusion), nothing should break. (3) If any new violations appear, they represent actual file write errors that should be handled — fix them with proper error checking or explicit '_ =' with a comment if truly safe to ignore. (4) Commit. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").
close_reason: Removed (*os.File).Write exclusion. Discovered os.Stdout.Write exclusion was inert (errcheck uses type matching). Fixed 4 callsites with explicit _ = assignments.

title: Remove dead errcheck exclusions: io.Copy and os.Setenv
description: The .golangci.yml errcheck exclude-functions list contains two entries with zero unchecked callsites in production code: io.Copy and os.Setenv. These are dead exclusions that should be removed so the linter catches future unchecked usage. Steps: (1) Remove 'io.Copy' and 'os.Setenv' from errcheck exclude-functions in .golangci.yml. (2) Run 'make check' (with dangerouslyDisableSandbox: true) to verify nothing breaks. (3) Commit. This project uses github.com/mieubrisse/stacktrace — never use bare 'return err', always wrap with stacktrace.Propagate(err, "meaningful message").
close_reason: Removed io.Copy and os.Setenv from errcheck exclusions. Fixed the 2 os.Setenv calls in tests by converting to t.Setenv.

title: Sort cron ls output by name for deterministic display
description: cron_ls.go:45 iterates over cfg.Crons (a Go map) without sorting. Output order changes between runs. Fix: collect map keys, sort them, then iterate in sorted order.

title: Remove dead Plist.WriteToDisk method
description: Plist.WriteToDisk() in plist.go:138-157 is never called. syncCronJob does its own inline atomic write (temp+rename). Having two write paths for the same operation risks divergence. Remove WriteToDisk entirely — the inline write in syncCronJob is the authoritative path.

title: Move cron management to server API endpoints
description: Cron operations (list, create, delete, enable/disable) currently read config.yml directly from the CLI. These should be server endpoints so the CLI only needs the socket. This is a subset of the broader effort to make the server the single owner of ~/.agenc/ file access (agenc-4ex2).
notes: Sibling of agenc-4ex2 — both are part of the goal to make the server the single owner of ~/.agenc/ file access.

title: Fix ptrToString helper in plist_test.go for multi-digit numbers
description: ptrToString uses rune arithmetic (string(rune(*p + '0'))) which only works for single-digit numbers. Values like 30, 14, 15 produce garbage Unicode. Use strconv.Itoa instead.

title: Use atomic WriteToDisk for plist writes in cron syncer
description: syncCronJob uses os.WriteFile directly but WriteToDisk (temp file + rename) exists and is safer against crash mid-write. Should use the atomic method.
close_reason: Replaced os.WriteFile with temp file + os.Rename for atomic plist writes

title: Fix JSON injection in cron source metadata
description: cron_syncer.go line 117 uses fmt.Sprintf for JSON with cron name — breaks if name contains double quote. Use json.Marshal instead.
close_reason: Replaced fmt.Sprintf with json.Marshal for source metadata in cron_syncer.go and cron_run.go

title: IsLoaded swallows all launchctl errors
description: launchd.Manager.IsLoaded always returns nil error. Permission denied or timeout from launchctl list is indistinguishable from 'not loaded'. Should parse error output and only treat 'service not found' as (false, nil).
close_reason: IsLoaded now captures launchctl output and only treats 'Could not find service' as not-loaded; other errors propagate

title: Extract setup and event handlers from Wrapper.Run
description: Wrapper.Run in internal/wrapper/wrapper.go has cyclomatic complexity 25. The function handles setup (logging, signal handling, goroutines) and the main event loop (select with 4 cases). Extract setup into a helper and move each event handler (handleSignal, handleCommand, handleClaudeExit) into separate methods. Target: ~12-15 for Run().
close_reason: Decomposed Wrapper.Run into setupRun, spawnClaude, handleSignal, handleClaudeExit. Run() now ~25 lines with tight select loop. All tests pass.

title: Extract phases from stash handlers to reduce complexity
description: handlePushStash (complexity 30) and handlePopStash (complexity 25) in internal/server/handle_stash.go are the most complex server handlers. Both mix precondition checks, data operations, tmux operations, and async coordination. Extract: checkStashPreconditions(), performStashCleanup() for push; resolveMostRecentStash(), restoreMission() for pop. Target: below 20 each.
close_reason: Decomposed handlePushStash into 5 helpers and handlePopStash into 4 helpers. Main handlers now ~25 lines each. Pure refactor, all tests pass.

title: Refactor ParseCronExpression to table-driven approach
description: ParseCronExpression in internal/launchd/plist.go has cyclomatic complexity 23 due to five identical parse-and-validate blocks (minute, hour, day, month, weekday). Use a table-driven approach: define a slice of field descriptors (name, min, max, special handling for weekday normalization), loop over fields. Would drop complexity to ~5-8.
close_reason: Refactored to table-driven with cronFieldDescriptor struct. 5 repetitive blocks → 1 loop. Net -22 lines. All tests pass.

title: Extract validators from ReadAgencConfig
description: ReadAgencConfig in internal/config/agenc_config.go has cyclomatic complexity 28 due to three nested validation loops (for repoConfigs, crons, paletteCommands). Extract into separate functions: validateRepoConfigs(), validateCrons(), validatePaletteCommands(). This would drop the main function to ~10-12 while making each validator focused and testable.
close_reason: Extracted validateRepoConfigs, validateCronConfigs, validatePaletteCommandConfigs from ReadAgencConfig. Pure refactor, all tests pass.

title: Convert TestMergeCredentialJSON to table-driven test
description: TestMergeCredentialJSON in internal/claudeconfig/merge_test.go has cyclomatic complexity 29 due to 12 sequential t.Run() blocks with nearly identical structure. Convert to a table-driven test (slice of test cases with a loop) to drop complexity to ~5-8 and improve readability.
close_reason: Converted to table-driven test with struct fields for error, changed, tokens, and custom checks. All 10 test cases preserved. 166→139 lines.

title: Refactor config update commands to reduce cyclomatic complexity
description: Three config update commands share the same pattern of repetitive field-update blocks that drive high cyclomatic complexity. Extract field-update helpers or use a table-driven approach to collapse the repetition. Functions: runConfigCronUpdate (cmd/config_cron_update.go, complexity 33), runConfigRepoConfigSet (cmd/config_repo_config_set.go, complexity 28), runConfigPaletteCommandUpdate (cmd/config_palette_command_update.go, complexity 27). All three have 5-7 nearly identical flag-check-validate-apply blocks.
close_reason: Created flag_helpers.go with applyStringFlag/applyBoolFlag/anyFlagChanged. Refactored all 3 config update commands: complexity 33→14, 28→15, 27→16. All tests pass.

title: Extract CLI flag/command constants to shared package
description: Move flag name and command name constants from cmd/command_str_consts.go to a shared internal package (e.g. internal/cli or internal/cmdconsts) so both cmd/ and internal/server/ can reference them without circular dependencies. Currently cron_syncer.go uses literal strings for flags like --headless, --source, --prompt, --blank because it can't import cmd/. After this change, all CLI string constants would be defined once and used everywhere.

title: Expand .gitignore to match go-cli-template best practices
description: The .gitignore is minimal (only /agenc, /dist/, beads files). The go-cli-template includes Go build artifacts (*.exe, *.dll, *.so, *.dylib, *.test, *.out), IDE files (.idea/, .vscode/, *.swp, *.swo), OS files (.DS_Store, Thumbs.db), and environment files (.env, .env.*). Adopt these entries.

title: Move config get/set/edit to server API endpoints
description: Currently config get/set/edit/unset commands read/write ~/.agenc/config/config.yml directly from the CLI process. This couples the CLI to the filesystem layout and requires the CLI to resolve agencDirpath for file access. These should instead be server API endpoints so the CLI only needs the server socket, consistent with how mission commands work. This would let the server be the single owner of ~/.agenc/ file access.

title: Remove deprecated cron_id and cron_name columns from missions table
description: The crons modernization (2026-03-18-crons-modernization-design.md) replaced the cron-specific cron_id and cron_name columns with generic source, source_id, and source_metadata columns. The old columns were left in place to avoid a disruptive SQLite table rebuild. This bead tracks their eventual removal. Requires a table rebuild migration (CREATE new table, INSERT INTO ... SELECT, DROP old table, ALTER TABLE RENAME).

title: Build auto-refinement loop
description: Build an automatic refinement loop into AgenC that continuously improves the system based on usage patterns and friction points.

title: Add mission provenance fields (spawn_context and spawned_by)
description:
Add two new fields to missions for better provenance tracking:

- spawn_context: UUID of the mission (if any) that the mission creator was looking at when they spawned this mission. This is a weak reference that provides context on what the user was doing before embarking on the new mission.
- spawned_by: UUID of the mission that actually spawned this sub-mission (if any). This is a hard reference, filled in only when a mission programmatically spawns a sub-mission.

The goal is to provide better mission provenance and help users figure out what they were doing before they embarked on their current mission.

title: Attach outcomes to missions
